seo.title: 100 Databases for AI Apps resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Databases for AI Apps resources for engineers.

content.intro: The shift toward AI-integrated applications has redefined database requirements, moving beyond traditional CRUD operations to include high-dimensional vector search and low-latency serverless connectivity. This resource focuses on the implementation of hybrid data models where relational integrity and semantic search coexist, emphasizing tools that support pgvector, edge-compatible drivers, and schema evolution for embeddings.

items.title: pgvector for PostgreSQL
items.description: The industry standard for adding vector similarity search to Postgres. Supports L2 distance, inner product, and cosine distance for embeddings.

items.title: Neon Serverless Postgres
items.description: Postgres implementation featuring storage/compute separation and an HTTP driver for accessing data from serverless environments without connection limits.

items.title: Supabase Vector
items.description: A managed pgvector environment that includes a client-side library for performing vector similarity searches directly from the frontend or edge functions.

items.title: Turso (libSQL)
items.description: SQLite-based edge database that supports vector extensions, optimized for low-latency access in globally distributed applications.

items.title: Cloudflare D1 Vector Search
items.description: Native vector search capabilities within Cloudflare's distributed SQLite database, allowing for semantic search at the edge.

items.title: MongoDB Atlas Vector Search
items.description: Enables vector search on unstructured BSON data, allowing developers to store metadata and embeddings in a single document.

items.title: RedisVL (Redis Vector Library)
items.description: A purpose-built library for using Redis as a vector database, ideal for high-speed semantic caching and session-based RAG.

items.title: PlanetScale Boost
items.description: A specialized query caching layer for MySQL that can be used to accelerate the retrieval of AI-related metadata and conversation histories.

items.title: ClickHouse Vector Search
items.description: High-performance analytical database used for massive-scale vector similarity searches where ingestion speed is a bottleneck.

items.title: TiDB Serverless Vector
items.description: MySQL-compatible distributed database with built-in vector search support, designed for scaling AI workloads horizontally.

sections.heading: Data Access, ORMs, and Schema Management

items.title: Drizzle ORM pgvector Extension
items.description: Provides TypeScript-first schema definitions for vector columns and helper functions for distance-based filtering.

items.title: Prisma $queryRaw for Vectors
items.description: Implementation pattern for executing vector similarity searches using raw SQL within Prisma while maintaining type safety for other models.

items.title: Kysely-pgvector
items.description: Type-safe SQL query builder integration for pgvector, allowing for complex joins between relational data and vector results.

items.title: Atlas Schema Migrations
items.description: A database schema management tool that supports HNSW and IVFFlat index declarations for pgvector in declarative HCL files.

items.title: TypeORM Vector Transformers
items.description: Using custom value transformers to map float arrays to Postgres vector types in NestJS or Node.js applications.

items.title: Prisma Accelerate
items.description: A global database cache and connection pooler that solves the connection exhaustion problem common in serverless AI apps.

items.title: SQLAlchemy pgvector (Python)
items.description: The primary integration for Python-based AI backends to manage vector storage within a relational SQLAlchemy model.

items.title: Drizzle Kit Migrations
items.description: CLI tool for generating SQL migrations that correctly handle adding vector dimensions and modifying HNSW index parameters.

items.title: EdgeDB AI
items.description: A graph-relational database that treats vectors as first-class citizens with a high-level query language (EdgeQL) for RAG.

items.title: PostgREST Vector RPCs
items.description: Exposing pgvector similarity functions as RESTful endpoints by wrapping them in Postgres stored procedures.

items.title: PgBouncer Configuration
items.description: Configuring transaction-mode pooling to handle the high volume of short-lived connections from AI worker processes.

items.title: Neon Serverless Driver
items.description: A WebSocket-based driver that allows querying Postgres over HTTP, bypassing TCP connection overhead in edge environments.

items.title: HNSW Index Tuning
items.description: Optimizing m and ef_construction parameters in pgvector to balance search speed against memory consumption.

items.title: IVFFlat Centroid Calculation
items.description: Strategy for determining the optimal number of lists for IVFFlat indexes based on total row count for faster search.

items.title: Semantic Caching with Redis
items.description: Using vector similarity to check if a similar prompt has been answered, reducing LLM API costs and database load.

items.title: Postgres Table Partitioning
items.description: Splitting large vector tables by time or metadata category to improve index maintenance and query performance.

items.title: Vector Quantization Techniques
items.description: Reducing the precision of embeddings (e.g., from float32 to int8) to decrease the storage footprint and memory usage in Postgres.

items.title: Supabase Hyperion
items.description: An optimized connection pooler designed specifically for massive scale in serverless Postgres environments.

items.title: Upstash Vector
items.description: A serverless vector database with an HTTP API, designed for use cases where zero-configuration and edge compatibility are required.

items.title: Zilliz Cloud (Managed Milvus)
items.description: Transition path for applications that outgrow pgvector, providing a dedicated engine for billion-scale vector search.

pro_tips: Always normalize your vectors to unit length (magnitude of 1.0) before insertion to use dot product distance, which is computationally cheaper than cosine distance.
Store original text or source metadata in a JSONB column within the same table as the vector to enable hybrid filtering in a single SQL query.
Wait until you have at least 1,000-5,000 rows before creating an IVFFlat or HNSW index; otherwise, sequential scans are often faster for small datasets.
Use a dedicated database schema for conversation memory with TTL (Time-To-Live) logic to prevent vector indexes from bloating with stale session data.
When using serverless functions, utilize HTTP-based database drivers instead of standard TCP drivers to avoid the 'too many connections' error during traffic spikes.
